text:
Once upon a time, in a small village nestled between whispering forests and the tranquil sea, there lived a curious hummingbird named Harold.

Harold was renowned across the land not just for his beautiful singing, but for something utterly unique. Whenever he flew from flower to flower, he collected stones.

Each stone he collected held a tiny story—a secret garden perhaps or a lost pet. One particularly special stone had a tiny, smiling cat on it. It never left Harold's feathered cloak.

One particularly sunny afternoon, as Harold flitted from blooming rose to blooming geranium, he noticed something unusual. His stone cat was staring at him with eyes brighter than any dawn. It had a story of its own.

The cat had fallen out of a magician's hat. It couldn't see well, but it liked Harold. The cat promised Harold tales when they met again.

That evening, Harold sat in the quiet garden by the sea. There were no stories that night. The cat had
